question_text: A light ray travelling through glass hits the glass-air boundary at an angle of incidence of 50°. The critical angle for glass is 42°. Will total internal reflection occur? Explain your answer.
correct_answer: Yes, total internal reflection will occur because the angle of incidence (50°) is greater than the critical angle (42°).
explanation: Total internal reflection only happens when the angle of incidence is greater than the critical angle. Since 50° > 42°, the light will bounce completely off the boundary instead of refracting through it.
